boxy_tools.description: Create a Google Calendar event in the selected calendar via the live API. This mutates external state and requires user approval before execution.

account_id.description: Optional Google account ID when multiple Google accounts are configured. Omit this to use the active account.
account_id.title: Account Id

attendees.description: Optional attendee email addresses for the new event.
attendees.title: Attendees

calendar_id.description: Optional calendar ID where the event should be created.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

description.description: Optional event description or agenda text.
description.title: Description

end.description: Event end time as an RFC 3339 timestamp.
end.title: End

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for orchestrator-provided deduplication and safe retries.
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key

location.description: Optional location string for the event.
location.title: Location

start.description: Event start time as an RFC 3339 timestamp.
start.title: Start

summary.description: Title of the calendar event to create.
summary.title: Summary

boxy_tools.name: google_calendar.calendar_create_event

boxy_tools.description: List Google Calendar events directly via Google. Prefer locally ingested Calendar queries unless the user explicitly asks for a live provider read.

account_id.description: Optional Google account ID when multiple Google accounts are configured. Omit this to use the active account.
account_id.title: Account Id

calendar_id.description: Optional calendar ID. Omit to use the primary calendar.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

limit.description: Maximum number of calendar events to return, between 1 and 250.
limit.title: Limit

page_token.description: Optional continuation token from a previous Calendar list response. Copy it exactly when requesting the next page.
page_token.title: Page Token

query.description: Optional free-text query for live Calendar events.
query.title: Query

time_max.description: Optional exclusive RFC 3339 upper time bound for live Calendar events.
time_max.title: Time Max

time_min.description: Optional inclusive RFC 3339 lower time bound for live Calendar events.
time_min.title: Time Min

boxy_tools.name: google_calendar.calendar_list_events

boxy_tools.description: List Google Chat messages directly via the Google Chat API. Provider page order follows Google defaults and is not reliable for recent/latest message summaries. Prefer locally ingested Chat message queries unless the user explicitly asks for a live provider read.

account_id.description: Optional Google account ID when multiple Google accounts are configured. Omit this to use the active account.
account_id.title: Account Id

limit.description: Maximum number of Chat messages to return, between 1 and 100.
limit.title: Limit

page_token.description: Optional continuation token from a previous Chat messages response. Copy it exactly when requesting the next page.
page_token.title: Page Token

space.description: Google Chat space name whose messages should be listed.
space.title: Space

boxy_tools.description: Dispatch a single-line WeChat plain-text message (max 4096 chars). Use the exact visible chat label that WeChat UI search should select first. Requires idempotency_key for safe deduplication/replay; `status=sent` is dispatch-only (not delivery-confirmed), and `unknown_delivery` requires retry with a new idempotency key. This mutates external state and requires user approval before execution.

display_id.description: Optional display id. Defaults to the connector display (typically 99).
display_id.title: Display Id

idempotency_key.description: Non-empty idempotency key for deduplication and safe replay.
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key

target.description: Non-empty exact visible WeChat chat label that UI search should select first.
target.title: Target

boxy_tools.name: wechat.send_message

boxy_tools.description: Send one WhatsApp text message via whatsapp-runtime. Uses idempotency_key as runtime clientMessageId and retries 409 conflicts using Retry-After. This mutates external state and requires user approval before execution.

idempotency_key.description: Non-empty idempotency key mapped to runtime clientMessageId for safe retries.
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key

target.description: Target phone number or chat JID accepted by whatsapp-runtime /messages/send.
target.title: Target

boxy_tools.name: whatsapp.send_message

builtin_tools.description: Execute simple local Python computations in a constrained environment. No network access and no general stdlib/runtime access; use it only for small local calculations and transformations. Do not use it to fetch network data, call Boxy tools, or replace connector or API reads.
builtin_tools.name: python_exec

builtin_tools.description: Search the public web for internet information. Do not use for private connector or locally ingested user data when a connector or local read tool already fits the request.
builtin_tools.name: web_search

before_contact_username.title: Before Contact Username

before_create_time.description: Optional seek cursor timestamp from a previous search_messages result.
before_create_time.title: Before Create Time

before_local_id.description: Optional seek cursor local message id from a previous search_messages result.
before_local_id.title: Before Local Id

contact_username.description: Optional exact WeChat contact username to restrict the message search.
contact_username.title: Contact Username

limit.description: Maximum number of WeChat search matches to return, between 1 and 200.
limit.title: Limit

query.description: Free-text keyword query for persisted WeChat messages.
query.title: Query

data_queries.name: wechat.search_messages

data_queries.description: Load recent message context for one WhatsApp chat from locally ingested data. Use this after you already know the chat_jid from a prior list or search result, and copy that chat_jid exactly. If the result is empty and there is no continuation token, treat that as no locally available message context for this chat rather than evidence that the chat was retrieved.

before_message_id.description: Optional cursor boundary from a prior chat_context result. Copy the previous `before_message_id` value exactly when continuing to older messages.
before_message_id.title: Before Message Id

before_ts_ms.description: Optional cursor boundary from a prior chat_context result. Copy the previous `before_ts_ms` value exactly when continuing to older messages.
before_ts_ms.title: Before Ts Ms

chat_jid.description: WhatsApp chat JID copied verbatim from a prior list or search result field such as `chats[].chat_jid`. Copy this exact value; never invent, normalize, or replace it with a display name, title hint, phone number, or email address.
chat_jid.title: Chat Jid

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp messages to return, between 1 and 200.
limit.title: Limit

data_queries.name: whatsapp.chat_context

data_queries.description: List the most recently active WhatsApp chats from locally ingested data. Use this to identify candidate conversations before fetching chat context. When you drill into one chat, copy the returned `chats[].chat_jid` value exactly into `whatsapp_chat_context`.

chat_jid.description: Optional exact chat JID filter for one WhatsApp chat row. When narrowing to one known chat, copy a prior result's `chat_jid` exactly.
chat_jid.title: Chat Jid

include_inactive.description: Whether inactive chats should be included in the result.
include_inactive.title: Include Inactive

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp chat rows to return, between 1 and 500.
limit.title: Limit

offset.description: Optional offset from a previous list_chats_latest page.
offset.title: Offset

query.description: Optional keyword filter applied to recent WhatsApp chats.
query.title: Query

data_queries.name: whatsapp.list_chats_latest

data_queries.description: List latest-state WhatsApp contacts from locally ingested data.

include_inactive.description: Whether inactive contacts should be included in the result.
include_inactive.title: Include Inactive

jid.description: Optional exact WhatsApp JID filter for one contact row.
jid.title: Jid

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp contacts to return, between 1 and 500.
limit.title: Limit

offset.description: Optional offset from a previous contacts page.
offset.title: Offset

query.description: Optional keyword filter applied to latest WhatsApp contacts.
query.title: Query

data_queries.name: whatsapp.list_contacts_latest

data_queries.description: List latest-state WhatsApp directory mappings resolved from locally ingested data.

chat_jid.description: Optional exact chat JID filter for one directory mapping row. When narrowing to one known chat, copy a prior result's `chat_jid` exactly.
chat_jid.title: Chat Jid

include_inactive.description: Whether inactive directory mappings should be included in the result.
include_inactive.title: Include Inactive

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp directory mappings to return, between 1 and 500.
limit.title: Limit

offset.description: Optional offset from a previous directory-mapping page.
offset.title: Offset

query.description: Optional keyword filter applied to latest directory mappings.
query.title: Query

data_queries.name: whatsapp.list_directory_mapping_latest

data_queries.description: Search the user's locally ingested WhatsApp chat history for exact words and phrases. Use this for lexical keyword search across chats or within one known chat_jid. When restricting the search to one chat, copy that `chat_jid` exactly from a prior result.

chat_jid.description: Optional chat JID to restrict the local search to one WhatsApp chat. Copy a prior result's `chat_jid` exactly; do not substitute display names, phone numbers, or email addresses.
chat_jid.title: Chat Jid

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp lexical search matches to return, between 1 and 100.
limit.title: Limit

text.description: Exact word or phrase query for locally ingested WhatsApp chat history.
text.title: Text

time_end_ms.description: Optional exclusive upper timestamp bound in milliseconds.
time_end_ms.title: Time End Ms

time_start_ms.description: Optional inclusive lower timestamp bound in milliseconds.
time_start_ms.title: Time Start Ms

data_queries.name: whatsapp.search_local_chat_shards

data_queries.description: Search the user's locally ingested WhatsApp chat history by semantic meaning. Use this when exact keywords are not enough, and restrict to one known `chat_jid` when you already have that exact value.

chat_jid.description: Optional chat JID to restrict the semantic search to one WhatsApp chat. Copy a prior result's `chat_jid` exactly; do not substitute display names, phone numbers, or email addresses.
chat_jid.title: Chat Jid

limit.description: Maximum number of WhatsApp semantic search matches to return, between 1 and 20.
limit.title: Limit

text.description: Semantic search query for locally ingested WhatsApp chat history.
text.title: Text

time_end_ms.description: Optional exclusive upper timestamp bound in milliseconds.
time_end_ms.title: Time End Ms

time_start_ms.description: Optional inclusive lower timestamp bound in milliseconds.
time_start_ms.title: Time Start Ms

data_queries.name: whatsapp.search_local_chat_shards_semantic
